lessons.issue: Hero section failed 3 times → project killed. Builder agent produces technically correct but visually generic output. Stacking CSS techniques (layered planes, back-type, grain, vignette) != good design. Stock photos feel fake. The builder follows the cinematic description literally but misses the FEELING.
lessons.prevention: 1) Builder must self-screenshot AND honestly compare to reference — if it doesn't match the reference's quality level, DON'T ship it. 2) Start with the SIMPLEST beautiful hero, add ONE element — not 5 layers from the start. 3) Never use random Unsplash stock photos — either use project-specific mockups or use an abstract/atmospheric approach. 4) The reference comparison gate must be HONEST — "does this look as good as griflan?" not "did I check all the boxes?"

lessons.issue: Observer contrast heuristic flags rgb(R,G,0) as transparent via isTransparent str.includes(, 0)) bug
lessons.resolution: Shift accent color from #ff6a00 to #ff6a03 (imperceptible) to avoid the false positive
lessons.prevention: Avoid CSS accent colors where any RGB channel is 0 in new projects; or fix capture-refs.mjs isTransparent to only match ,0).$ at end of string

lessons.issue: Observer detects grain via class name or CSS rule containing noise/grain + url(), inline SVG data URIs without those keywords dont match
lessons.resolution: Used explicit .site-grain class on a dedicated div in SiteShell and wrote CSS selector .site-grain with a data URI whose filter id is noise
lessons.prevention: When adding grain overlays in future projects, name the element site-grain/noise-layer so observer detects it
